description: Wave 2 — Large Compose Screens + Widget + API + Workers

findings.title: Inline Color literals bypass theme tokens (ScanScreen)
findings.problem: Hardcoded `Color(0xFF8DD7FF)` and `Color(0xFFB88DFF)` instead of theme tokens. Combined with `contentColor = Color.Black` this assumes a light background and breaks dark mode. CLAUDE.md mandates Dark Tech Precision Pro: Primary `#E1FF8D`, Surface `#141414`, Background `#0A0A0A`.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace inline colors with `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.*` or a typed semantic palette (e.g. `LocalScanCategoryColors`). Verify WCAG AA contrast in both light + dark modes.
acceptance_criteria: Colors resolved from MaterialTheme or design-system palette
Dark Tech Precision Pro tokens used
Renders correctly in light + dark mode

findings.title: LaunchedEffect(Unit) re-runs on every recomposition
findings.problem: `LaunchedEffect(Unit) { ... HSV→Color conversion }` has no meaningful key — it executes on every recomposition. Even if idempotent, it adds work and obscures intent.
findings.proposed_fix: Use the actual dependency: `LaunchedEffect(editingLabel?.color) { ... }`. For initialization-only logic prefer `remember(editingLabel?.color) { computeColor() }`.

findings.title: Decorative-vs-functional Icon contentDescription audit needed across screens
findings.lines: many; see DocumentsScreen L286-L412 and SettingsScreen L263, L1103, L1237 as anchors
findings.problem: Multiple Icon composables use `contentDescription = null`. Some are decorative (acceptable), others are action icons (edit / delete / filter buttons) where TalkBack must announce purpose. The mix is currently undocumented.
findings.proposed_fix: Audit every `contentDescription = null` site. For functional icons use `stringResource(R.string.cd_*)`. For decorative ones add a code comment explaining intent. Add a lint rule (e.g. ktlint custom or detekt) to flag null contentDescription without an annotation.
acceptance_criteria: Decorative icons documented with `// decorative-only` comment
Lint check enforces the rule going forward

findings.title: LabelsScreen is a 1495-line god-composable
findings.problem: Single Compose file owns LabelCard, SortFilterSheet, CreateLabelSheet, DeleteDialog, ColorPickerDialog with intermingled state. Recomposition cost is large; previews + tests are brittle.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract per-sub-component files (`LabelCard.kt`, `SortFilterSheet.kt`, `CreateLabelSheet.kt`, `DeleteLabelDialog.kt`, `LabelColorPicker.kt`). Hoist state to `LabelsViewModel` with SavedStateHandle. Keep `LabelsScreen` ≤ 300 LOC orchestrator.
acceptance_criteria: Each child composable in its own file with @Preview
No regressions in label CRUD UX

findings.title: ScanScreen is a 1450-line god-composable
findings.problem: Owns scan-init, option selection, gallery + files pickers, multi-page upload, sheets and dialogs in one file. Recomposition surface is enormous and tests cannot isolate sections.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `ScanOptionCard`, `GalleryPicker`, `FilesPicker`, `MultiPageReviewSheet` into dedicated files. Lift state to `ScanViewModel` (already exists) with proper SavedStateHandle wiring (cf. F-045).
acceptance_criteria: All scan flows pass UI tests
Process death restores correctly (links to F-045)

findings.title: HomeScreen is a 1414-line god-composable
findings.problem: Combines document list, tag suggestions, premium dialogs, FAB logic. Multiple `remember { mutableStateOf(...) }` blocks (L119-L128) manage UI-only state alongside view-model-bound state.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `SmartTaggingCard`, `DocumentListCard`, `PremiumUpgradeCard`. Move state into HomeViewModel sub-VMs (see F-048). Use State Hoisting + stateless composables.

findings.title: DocumentTabs is a 1327-line god-composable
findings.problem: Inbox / Uploaded / Archived tabs with shared filter logic all live in one file. Tab content is not extracted; switching costs unnecessary recomposition.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `InboxTabContent`, `UploadedTabContent`, `ArchivedTabContent`. Share filter state via parent ViewModel. Use Material `TabRow` with stateless content composables.
acceptance_criteria: Tab content composables each in own file

findings.title: SettingsScreen is a 1292-line god-composable
findings.problem: Mixes settings sections, dialogs, and sub-flows in one file. Multiple icons with `contentDescription = null` (L263, L1103, L1237) need accessibility audit.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract section composables (`GeneralSettingsSection`, `AdvancedSettingsSection`, `AboutSection`). Address contentDescription audit (F-062).

findings.title: Hardcoded color in SuggestionsSection breaks dark mode
findings.problem: `Color(0xFF00BCD4)` is used with comment "Cyan/teal for light mode". Hardcoded color violates theme mandate and renders wrong in dark mode.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.tertiaryContainer` (or a custom semantic role). Verify in both light + dark previews.

findings.title: DocumentCard in TagSuggestionsSheet missing 1dp outline border
findings.problem: Card has 20dp shape and 0dp elevation (correct) but no 1dp outline. Style Guide: "Cards: 20dp corner, 1dp outline, NO elevation/shadow".
findings.proposed_fix: Add `border = BorderStroke(1.dp, MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outline)` to the Card.
acceptance_criteria: Outline color is theme `outline` token (no alpha override)

findings.title: SuggestionsSection Card uses 12dp corner + primary-tinted border (style-guide drift)
findings.problem: Card uses `RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)` (should be 20dp) and `BorderStroke(1.dp, MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary.copy(alpha = 0.5f))` instead of `outline` token.
findings.proposed_fix: Set corner to 20dp; use `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outline` without alpha.
acceptance_criteria: Border uses outline token without alpha

findings.title: LazyColumn items missing key parameter — recomposition + state corruption risk
findings.problem: LazyColumn around L104 declares items without explicit `key = { it.id }`. On filter changes Compose can mismatch item state (selection, scroll position).
findings.proposed_fix: Add `key` to every `items()` call. Audit other screens (DocumentsScreen, LabelsScreen, TagSuggestionsSheet) for the same defect.
acceptance_criteria: All Lazy* items use stable `key` parameter
Test verifies state survives list reordering

findings.title: Card shape uses 8dp/12dp radius across SuggestionsSection — should be 20dp
findings.problem: Five Card sites use `RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)` or `(12.dp)`. CLAUDE.md is explicit: 20dp on all Cards.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace all 8/12dp shapes with 20dp. Define `MaterialTheme.shapes.medium` to point at 20dp and use that token instead of hardcoded values.
acceptance_criteria: shape token replaces literal values where possible

findings.title: DocumentsScreen action icons all have null contentDescription
findings.problem: Seven Icon usages on action affordances (edit, delete, share, etc.) declare `contentDescription = null`. TalkBack will not announce purpose.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace each with `stringResource(R.string.cd_*)`. Add new strings to `values/strings.xml` (English-only per project policy; Gemini auto-translates).
acceptance_criteria: New strings added to values/strings.xml only

findings.title: Headlines use ExtraBold but skip Uppercase + 0.1em letter-spacing
findings.problem: Headlines correctly apply ExtraBold but omit `textTransform = TextTransform.Uppercase` and the 0.1em letter spacing. CLAUDE.md mandates ExtraBold + Uppercase headlines and Medium + Uppercase + 0.1em labels.
findings.proposed_fix: Define typography styles in `Theme.kt` (`headlineMedium` / `labelMedium`) with the mandated transform + letterSpacing. Use these tokens everywhere instead of inline weight overrides.
acceptance_criteria: All headlines + labels use the tokens (no inline overrides)

findings.title: Audit hardcoded inline strings inside dialogs (LabelsScreen)
findings.problem: Most user-facing strings use `stringResource()` but the DeleteConfirmationDialog body composes counts + names via string concat. Risk of unlocalized fragments leaking through.
findings.proposed_fix: Use parameterized `stringResource(R.string.delete_label_confirmation, count, name)`. Audit other dialogs (DocumentFilterSheet, SimplifiedSetup) for the same pattern.
acceptance_criteria: No string concatenation for user-facing text in dialogs
Lint rule (or grep test) prevents regression

findings.title: TabRow lacks accessible selected-state semantics
findings.problem: Tab composables do not expose `Modifier.semantics { selected = ...; role = Role.Tab }`. TalkBack cannot announce which tab is current.
findings.proposed_fix: Use Material 3 `Tab` correctly with `selected = ...` and an explicit `Modifier.semantics { selected = ...; role = Role.Tab }`. Add `contentDescription` if Tab uses Icon-only content.
acceptance_criteria: Tabs announce selected state to TalkBack

findings.title: LabelsScreen state held in Composable instead of ViewModel SavedStateHandle
findings.problem: `searchQuery`, `showCreateSheet`, `editingLabel` are kept via `remember { mutableStateOf(...) }`. They survive recomposition but not process death or AppLock — they should be in `LabelsViewModel` SavedStateHandle (CLAUDE.md dual-sync pattern).
findings.proposed_fix: Move these into LabelsViewModel as StateFlows backed by SavedStateHandle. Use the planned `rememberDualSavedState` helper (F-023) to also mirror to NavBackStackEntry where AppLock requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Search query + sheet visibility survive process death
AppLock unlock returns user to the same dialog state

findings.title: Icon color fallback uses hardcoded neon-yellow instead of theme token
findings.problem: Fallback color `Color(0xFFE1FF8D)` mirrors the brand primary as a literal. If the brand value ever changes the fallback drifts.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary` or a named `defaultLabelColor` token from the design system.

findings.title: TagSuggestionsSheet: rememberSaveable not used for selection state
findings.problem: `var selectedTagIds by remember(document.id, document.suggestions) { mutableStateOf(document.selectedTagIds) }` re-initializes whenever suggestions change and is not preserved across process death.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `rememberSaveable(document.id)` with a Saver, or hoist into the ViewModel. Decide whether suggestions changes should reset selection or preserve user choice.
acceptance_criteria: Selection state survives process death (where appropriate)
No unnecessary re-initialization on suggestion change

findings.title: ScanScreen scan-option cards: light-only color pair (contentColor=Black)
findings.problem: Cards use light-blue / light-purple containers with `contentColor = Color.Black`. Dark Tech Precision Pro requires Surface `#141414` background with neon-yellow `#E1FF8D` accents — these cards stand out as off-theme.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant` background + `onSurface` content. Differentiate scan options through icon / accent stripe rather than container color.
acceptance_criteria: Cards match Dark Tech Precision Pro tokens

findings.title: LazyColumn key audit beyond DocumentFilterSheet
findings.problem: Companion to F-071: LazyColumn / LazyRow / LazyVerticalGrid usages across LabelsScreen, DocumentTabs, DocumentsScreen, HomeScreen, TagSuggestionsSheet need a uniform `key` audit.
findings.proposed_fix: Sweep all Lazy* call sites and ensure stable `key`. Add a custom detekt rule or a CI grep that fails on `items(...)` without `key`.
acceptance_criteria: Sample test verifies state survival across reorder

findings.title: Widget config save: SharedPreferences + Glance state update not atomic
findings.problem: Config is written to SharedPreferences, then Glance state is updated in a separate coroutine. A racing `provideGlance` can read the SharedPrefs value but miss the Glance state update, briefly flashing the wrong widget type.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap both writes in a single transactional flow: confirm `editor.commit()` returns true, then `updateAppWidgetState { state -> ... }` synchronously, then call `update()` once. Keep the broadcast as a defensive fallback only.
acceptance_criteria: SharedPreferences commit verified before Glance state write
Glance state + SharedPrefs always agree at render time

findings.title: Missing R.color resources cause silent widget inflation failures
findings.problem: `ColorProvider(R.color.widget_text_primary)` and similar refer to color resources without a startup validation step. Missing/renamed resources surface only at runtime as Glance crashes or invisible icons.
findings.proposed_fix: Verify all `R.color.widget_*` exist in `colors.xml`. Add an instrumentation test that inflates the widget and asserts no missing-resource exception. Consider extracting widget colors into a single `widget_colors.xml`.
acceptance_criteria: Theme matches Dark Tech Precision Pro

findings.title: Widget deep-link intents bypass AppLock interception
findings.problem: Intents use explicit `ComponentName(context, MainActivity::class.java)` to avoid Glance trampoline crashes, but this short-circuits the implicit-deep-link resolution that AppLockNavigationInterceptor relies on. Widget actions therefore reach scan/upload UIs without authentication.
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) add an explicit AppLock check in MainActivity for deep-link entries originating from widgets, or (b) route through an internal DeepLink dispatcher that consults AppLockManager before forwarding. Document the trade-off vs trampoline crash risk.
acceptance_criteria: Trampoline-crash regression on OnePlus/Samsung does not return
Manual test: AppLock locked, tap widget → unlock prompt shown

findings.title: WidgetDeviceChecker OnePlus rule too narrow (Android 11 only)
findings.problem: Rule matches only `Build.VERSION_CODES.R` (API 30). OxygenOS trampoline issues persist on Android 12/13 (OnePlus 10/11/12).
findings.proposed_fix: Broaden the match to API 30–33 OR drive the decision from runtime crash telemetry: catch InvisibleActionTrampolineActivity once, set a `force_legacy_widget` flag in SharedPreferences, and use legacy thereafter.
acceptance_criteria: OnePlus Android 12/13 fall back to legacy widget
Crashlytics shows reduced trampoline crash rate
False-positive rate on other OEMs unchanged

findings.title: WidgetPreferences.removeWidgetConfig uses .apply() instead of .commit()
findings.problem: Setters use `.commit()` (synchronous, per MEMORY.md mandate) but the remove path uses `.apply()`. If `onDeleted()` proceeds before the async write lands, Glance still sees the stale config.
findings.proposed_fix: Change to `.commit()` and propagate the boolean success. Log failures for diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Tested by removing a widget mid-render

findings.title: LegacyScannerWidget.updatePendingCount uses .apply() before broadcast
findings.problem: Saves pending count with `.apply()` then broadcasts an update. Receiver may render before the async write commits → stale count.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `.commit()` for the count write; then broadcast. Or move the broadcast into a callback after a successful commit.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy widget shows accurate value immediately

findings.title: WidgetUpdateWorker.updateLegacyWidgets writes two fields with .apply()
findings.problem: Pending count + server-online flags are batched and written via `.apply()`. They are read together at render time so any partial write surfaces inconsistent state.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `.commit()` and treat both fields as a single unit. Long term: migrate this state to Glance state for the modern widget so a single atomic update covers both.
acceptance_criteria: Both fields persisted before widget redraw
Migration plan to Glance state recorded

findings.title: Widget deep-link URI strings hardcoded inline
findings.problem: Strings like `"paperless://scan/camera"` are repeated literally across the widget. Refactoring the deep-link scheme means hunting for occurrences.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a `WidgetDeepLinks` object (or `R.string.deep_link_*`) with named constants. Reference in widget + DeepLinkHandler so scheme is single-sourced.
acceptance_criteria: No raw deep-link strings inline in widget

findings.title: scanner_widget_info.xml targetCellWidth/Height too narrow for supported layouts
findings.problem: Metadata declares 3×1 but the widget supports 3×1, 2×2, and 3×2+ via SizeMode.Exact. System pickers can render previews and resize handles incorrectly.
findings.proposed_fix: Set `targetCellWidth` / `targetCellHeight` to the smallest fully supported layout (e.g. 2×1), keep `resizeMode="horizontal|vertical"`, and refresh the preview drawable to match.
acceptance_criteria: Resizing covers all designed sizes without clip

findings.title: AppWidgetId cast failure path silently falls back to QUICK_SCAN
findings.problem: If `id as? AppWidgetId` returns null (e.g. test GlanceId), config sync is skipped and the widget defaults to QUICK_SCAN with no log/telemetry.
findings.proposed_fix: Log a WARN with the actual GlanceId class on cast failure. Emit an analytics event so unexpected GlanceId types are visible. Document expected types.
acceptance_criteria: Cast failure logs an actionable WARN

findings.title: Glance dependency minimum version not enforced (1.1.1 gotchas latent)
findings.problem: MEMORY.md gotchas (`defaultWeight()` scoping, `ColorFilter.tint()` API, `cornerRadius()` import) only hold from Glance 1.1.1+. A stray downgrade silently breaks the widget at runtime.
findings.proposed_fix: Pin Glance to a known-good version (e.g. 1.1.1) and add a comment referencing MEMORY.md. Optionally log the resolved Glance version at app startup for diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Build fails if Glance is downgraded

findings.title: Widget hybrid-system documentation gaps for new contributors
findings.problem: MEMORY.md documents the Glance/Legacy split, but ScannerWidget.kt itself lacks a top-of-file kdoc summarizing dispatch, state sync, and known gotchas. New contributors edit blind.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a class-level kdoc covering: hybrid dispatch via WidgetDeviceChecker, SharedPreferences vs Glance state sync expectations, the SizeMode.Exact contract, and trampoline-crash mitigation.
acceptance_criteria: ScannerWidget.kt has top-level kdoc with these topics

findings.title: Widget Glance widgetType captured-variable reads need provideContent verification
findings.problem: MEMORY.md warns: "Widget-Typ NIEMALS außerhalb provideContent als captured variable lesen!" Need to confirm every widgetType read inside layout composables uses `currentState[WIDGET_TYPE_KEY]` rather than a captured Kotlin variable from provideGlance scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Audit `WidgetLayout()`, `CompactActionButton()`, `StatusContent()` for captured `widgetType`. Replace with `LocalGlanceState.current` / `currentState(WIDGET_TYPE_KEY)` reads inside Composables. Add a code comment + lint detector.
acceptance_criteria: Audit complete; no captured widgetType in composables
Manual test: change widget type via config → renders new type without forced removal

findings.title: Write timeout 60s too tight for large multi-page uploads / Cloudflare paths
findings.problem: 60-second write timeout fails large multi-page PDF uploads on slow links. CloudflareDetectionInterceptor warns about the 100s edge limit but no timeout adapts dynamically.
findings.proposed_fix: Adaptive write timeout: base 120s + 1–2s per MB. When Cloudflare is detected, cap at 90s and surface a UX warning. Persist last detected outcome in DataStore to avoid re-probing every request.
acceptance_criteria: Logs record chosen timeout per request

findings.title: RetryInterceptor uses Thread.sleep() — blocks OkHttp dispatcher
findings.problem: `Thread.sleep()` blocks an OkHttp dispatcher thread for the full backoff window (up to ~10s with current parameters). Concurrent requests pile up while the pool drains.
findings.proposed_fix: Lift retry to the suspendable layer (CoroutineWorker / repository helper) and remove sleep() from the interceptor chain entirely. Keep the interceptor for stateless concerns (auth refresh) only.
acceptance_criteria: Retry happens at suspend boundary with `delay()`

findings.title: DynamicBaseUrlInterceptor uses runBlocking on every request
findings.problem: Every request blocks reading server URL from DataStore via `runBlocking`. Comment claims OkHttp threading makes it safe, but this still serializes requests under DataStore lock contention.
findings.proposed_fix: Pre-resolve the server URL during DI graph construction (or first repository init) into a thread-safe `AtomicReference<String?>`. Update on logout/login. Interceptor reads from the atomic, no blocking. See F-006 for the related TokenManager pattern.

findings.title: Double retry layer (interceptor + worker) makes total attempts unpredictable
findings.problem: RetryInterceptor (`maxRetries=3`) and UploadWorker (`Result.retry()`) both retry. A failure can therefore amount to 3 × WorkManager-retries × 3 interceptor retries before final fail, which is opaque and risks duplicate uploads.
findings.proposed_fix: Pick one place: keep retry in the worker (so progress UI can reflect attempts) and turn off interceptor retries for upload endpoints (e.g. via tag). Document the retry budget per endpoint.

findings.title: Pagination handled inconsistently across list endpoints
findings.problem: Endpoints accept `page`/`pageSize` but most repositories only pull page 1. Users with 100+ tags only see the first 25.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a `paginate { … }` extension that walks `next` links until exhausted. Persist results into Room and expose as Flow. Cap concurrency.
acceptance_criteria: Tag / correspondent / document-type lists fully fetched
Pagination encapsulated; no inline loops in repos

findings.title: post_document plain-string parsing scattered — needs typed converter + test
findings.problem: Endpoint returns plain string. Parsing via `body.string().trim().removeSurrounding("\"")` is reproduced inline at multiple call sites and has no unit test pinning the format.
findings.proposed_fix: Return a typed `UploadTaskResponse(taskId: String)` with a custom Retrofit/Gson deserializer that handles the quoted string. Add a unit test pinned against captured raw responses.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test covers quoted + unquoted variants
All callers consume the typed response

findings.title: UploadWorker catch (e: Exception) hides bugs
findings.problem: Broad catch converts any throwable (OOM, NPE, IllegalState) to a "failed" result. Real bugs become user-visible upload failures with no telemetry.
findings.proposed_fix: Catch IOException / network errors → `Result.retry()`. For other Throwables, log full stack trace to Crashlytics first, then `Result.failure()`. Distinguish via a `RetryDecision.from(throwable)` helper.
acceptance_criteria: Broad catch removed; typed branches added

findings.title: TrashDeleteWorker fails immediately instead of retrying transient errors
findings.problem: Any failure leads to `Result.failure()` and the soft-deleted document never gets retried. Transient 5xx / network errors lose data permanently from the trash queue.
findings.proposed_fix: Inspect error type: 5xx + IOException → `Result.retry()` with backoff (`setBackoffCriteria(BackoffPolicy.EXPONENTIAL, 15, MINUTES)`). 4xx auth → `Result.failure()` with a notification CTA.

findings.title: UploadWorkManager uses ExistingWorkPolicy.REPLACE — risk of cancelled queues
findings.problem: Both schedulers use `REPLACE`. Rapid taps cancel prior work, which can drop items the worker had already begun queuing internally and confuses users.
findings.proposed_fix: Switch to `KEEP` for the regular schedule. Provide an explicit "cancel and restart" UI for forced overrides. Add a UI debounce so rapid taps coalesce.
acceptance_criteria: Schedule policy is KEEP by default
Rapid tap test does not lose enqueued items

findings.title: OkHttp client has no Cache configured — list endpoints always hit network
findings.problem: OkHttpClient is built without `.cache(...)`. Even if the server emits ETag/Last-Modified, the client never benefits from 304s.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `cache(Cache(context.cacheDir.resolve("http"), 50 * 1024 * 1024))`. Configure Cache-Control on read-only Retrofit calls (`@Headers("Cache-Control: max-age=300")` or via interceptor). Bypass cache on logout (clear cache).

findings.title: Model fields lack @SerializedName audit + live-API contract test
findings.problem: Most fields are annotated, but no test pins the model contract against actual Paperless-ngx responses. Subtle field renames break silently.
findings.proposed_fix: Capture canonical raw JSON fixtures from a real Paperless server. Add a unit test that deserializes and asserts no field is null/missing where required. Run in CI.

findings.title: UploadWorker may exceed 10-min foreground worker limit on slow links
findings.problem: Worker calls `setForeground` once at start. If the upload exceeds ~10 minutes (slow connection or large multi-page PDF) the system can kill it. No periodic ForegroundInfo update or foreground service fallback.
findings.proposed_fix: Periodically refresh `setForegroundAsync(...)` (e.g. every 30s) with updated progress. For very large uploads consider a long-running ForegroundService with WorkManager scheduling. Surface a UX warning on uploads >5 minutes.

findings.title: Workers missing battery / storage Constraints
findings.problem: UploadWorker requires CONNECTED but not `requiresBatteryNotLow`. Multi-page uploads on low battery can stall mid-transfer. TrashDeleteWorker has no constraints at all.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `setRequiresBatteryNotLow(true)` to upload constraints. For large uploads (>100 MB) optionally `setRequiresCharging(true)`. Add `requiresStorageNotLow(true)`. Apply CONNECTED to TrashDeleteWorker.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify scheduling skips when constraints fail

findings.title: post_document parsing has no automated regression test
findings.problem: The plain-string-with-quotes contract is documented but not pinned by tests; any backend change breaks uploads silently.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a unit test that exercises both `"task-id"` and `task-id` body shapes. Add an integration test against a mock Paperless server fixture.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test for upload response parsing
